---
title: "Markov HW 5"
author: "Kathryn Saunders"
date: "2026-03-10"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=TRUE}
library(tidyverse) #for graphs and useful functions
library(knitr) #to knit to pdf
library(tinytex) #for LaTeX

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(out.width = "60%",fig.align='center')
```

#Problem 2

b.
```{r}
library(pracma)

#Parameters
a <- 0.04
b <- 0.16
K <- 0.1

p1 <- K * exp(a * 1)
p2 <- K * exp(a * 2)
p3 <- K * exp(a * 3)
p4 <- K * exp(a * 4)

q2 <- K * exp(b * 1)
q3 <- K * exp(b * 2)
q4 <- K * exp(b * 3)
q5 <- K * exp(b * 4)

#Matrix
p_matrix <- matrix(c(
  1 - p1, p1, 0, 0, 0,
  q2, 1 - p2 - q2, p2, 0, 0,
  0, q3, 1 - p3 - q3, p3, 0,
  0, 0, q4, 1 - p4 - q4, p4,
  0, 0, 0, q5, 1 - q5
), nrow=5, byrow=TRUE)

#Find eigenvector with eigenvalue close to 1
eig <- eigen(t(p_matrix))
eigx <- which(abs(eig$values - 1) < 1e-8)
pi_vec <- Re(eig$vectors[, eigx])

#Normalize
pi_vec <- pi_vec / sum(pi_vec)
pi_vec

#Check
sum(pi_vec)

```

c.
```{r}
set.seed(123)

#Parameters
n_steps <- 1e6
states <- 1:5
current_state <- sample(states, 1)

#Markov chain loop
for(t in 1:n_steps){
  states[t] <- current_state
  current_state <- sample(1:5, 1, prob=p_matrix[current_state, ])
}

#Calculate stationary distribution
sim_dist <- table(states) / n_steps
sim_dist

#Plot
barplot(sim_dist, names.arg=1:5, main="Fraction of time in state i")

```

d. 
```{r}
#Theoretical
pi_part <- exp((a-b) * (0:4 * 1:5 / 2))
pi_theory <- pi_part / sum(pi_part)

#Plot
barplot(rbind(sim_dist, pi_vec, pi_theory), beside=TRUE, 
        col=c("blue", "red", "purple"), names.arg=1:5,
        main="Simulated vs Theoretical Distributions",
        legend=c("Simulated", "Eigenvector", "Detailed Balance"))

```

#Problem 3

b.
```{r}
#Running Python virtually 
## (I couldn't figure out how to solve a matrix symbolically in R)
library(reticulate)
sympy <- import("sympy")
sympy

#Declare 'a' as a symbolic variable
a2 <- sympy$Symbol("a", real=TRUE)

#Matrix
A <- sympy$Matrix(list(
  list(1-a2, a2, 0),
  list(a2, 0, 1-a2),
  list(0, 1-a2, a2)
))

#Compute eigenvalues and eigenvectors
eigenvals = A$eigenvals()
eigenvects = A$eigenvects()

cat("The eigenvalues are:\n")
for (val in names(eigenvals)) {
  cat(sympy$sstr(sympy$sympify(val)), "\n")
}

cat("\nThe eigenvectors are:\n")
for (ev in eigenvects) {
  cat("\nEigenvalue:", sympy$sstr(ev[[1]]), "\n")
  cat("Eigenvector:\n")
  print(ev[[3]][[1]])
}

```
c.
```{r}
a3 <- 0.99
n <- 0:500

A3 <- matrix(c(
  1-a3, a3, 0,
  a3, 0, 1-a3,
  0, 1-a3, a3
), nrow=3, byrow=TRUE)

#Decomposition
eig <- eigen(A3)
eigenvals2 <- eig$values
eigenvects2 <- eig$vectors

q0 <- c(1, 0, 0)

#Compute q0
c <- t(eigenvects2) %*% q0

#Compute qn
qn_list <- matrix(0, nrow=length(n), ncol=3)

for (i in 1:length(n)) {
  qn_list[i, ] <- (eigenvects2 %*% (c * eigenvals2^n[i]))[,1]
}

#Processing for plot - it looked strange in base R
df <- data.frame(n = n,
  q1 = qn_list[,1],
  q2 = qn_list[,2],
  q3 = qn_list[,3]
)

df_long <- pivot_longer(df, cols = c(q1, q2, q3),
                        names_to = "state", values_to = "value")

#Plot
ggplot(df_long, aes(x = n, y = value, color = state)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1/3, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", size = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "q_n Over Time",
    subtitle = "The horizontal dashed line represents the stationary distribution",
    x = "n (time step)",
    y = "Probability"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

e.
```{r}
library(expm)
N_list <- c(100, 1000, 10000)
steps <- 400

#Theoretical
qn_theory <- sapply(0:steps, function(n) {
  (expm::`%^%`(A3, n) %*% c(1,0,0))[1]
})


#Simulation
run_simulation <- function(N) {
  sim_states <- matrix(1, N, steps + 1)
  for (i in 1:N) {
    for (j in 1:steps) {
      current_state <- sim_states[i, j]
      sim_states[i, j + 1] <- sample(1:3, 1, prob = A3[current_state, ])
    }
  }
  colMeans(sim_states == 1)
}

#Plot
plot_results <- function(N, main_title) {
  fn <- run_simulation(N)
  plot(0:steps, fn, type='l', col='red', lwd=2, ylim=c(0,1),
     ylab='Probability in State 1', xlab='Number of steps', 
     main=paste('N=', N), xlim=c(0, steps))
  lines(0:steps, qn_theory, col='blue', lwd=2, lty=2)
  legend("topright", legend=c("Simulation", "Theory"), col=c("red", "blue"), lwd=2, lty=1:2)
}

par(mfrow=c(3,1))
for (N in N_list) {
  plot_results(N, paste("Number of chains =", N))
}
```
